title: 10 best herbal tinctures for burping
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for burping are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants that help alleviate excessive burping, a common yet uncomfortable digestive issue. These tinctures work by reducing gas and relaxing the digestive system. The best herbal tinctures for burping include Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinnamomum verum, Carum carvi, Mentha x piperita, Cinchona officinalis, Pimpinella anisum, Curcuma longa, and Angelica archangelica. If left untreated, excessive burping can lead to social embarrassment, discomfort, and even digestive problems, negatively impacting one's quality of life, work, and relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with burping because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. When we eat, our body breaks down food into smaller particles, and sometimes these particles can be difficult for our body to digest. Ginger tincture helps to stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach, which in turn reduces burping. Additionally, ginger has natural carminative properties that help to release trapped gases in the digestive system, further reducing the occurrence of burping.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of bloating and discomfort associated with burping.

Digestive enzymes: The enzymes present in ginger, such as zingibain, can stimulate digestion and improve the breakdown of nutrients, reducing the likelihood of undigested food particles causing gas and subsequent burping.
Carminative properties: Ginger's carminative properties help to expel trapped gases from the digestive tract, relieving symptoms of bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with burping.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and relaxant properties, which can help alleviate digestive discomfort and reduce the frequency of burping.

Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols are also phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the digestive system and alleviate symptoms associated with burping.

Terpenes (specifically, B-zingiberene and bisabolol): These terpenes have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms associated with burping, such as discomfort and bloating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part, as they contain the highest concentration of gingerol, the compound responsible for its medicinal properties.
Roots: They are also used to make tinctures, as they contain a lower concentration of gingerol compared to the rhyzomes, but still have some medicinal properties.
Stems: They are sometimes used to make tinctures, although they contain even lower concentrations of gingerol compared to the roots and rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the late summer or early fall season for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a single layer on paper bags or screens for 2 weeks.
Chop the dried rhizomes into small pieces and weigh out 250 grams for the tincture.
Combine the chopped rhizomes with 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks with occasional shaking and strain it through cheesecloth after the steeping period.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with burping because its essential oil, menthol, has a calming effect on the digestive system. It relaxes the muscles in the stomach and esophagus, reducing spasms that can cause excessive belching. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the digestive tract, reducing irritation and inflammation that may contribute to burping. By calming and soothing the digestive system, peppermint tinctures provide relief from bothersome belches and discomfort associated with indigestion and bloating.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Peppermint tincture relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to excessive belching.

Digestive stimulant property: Peppermint tincture stimulates digestive enzymes and improves gut motility, helping to break down food more efficiently and reduce the likelihood of gas buildup and burping.
Carminative property: Peppermint tincture releases trapped gases in the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort that can lead to excessive belching.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps to reduce burping by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing the frequency of gas release.
Menthone: Menthone has antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate digestive discomfort and reduce the occurrence of burping.
Eugenol: Eugenol, a phenolic compound, has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and helping to alleviate symptoms of burping and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of menthol, a natural antispasmodic that helps relieve gas and bloating.
Stems: Used for their menthol content, which aids in relaxing muscles and reducing discomfort associated with burping.
Roots: Employed for their medicinal properties, including their ability to soothe digestive issues and help alleviate gas and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar.
Combine the gathered mentha x piperita with 2 parts of vodka in the glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid from the jar using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Bottle the strained liquid in dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with burping because of its natural ability to soothe digestive issues. The herbal extract has a calming effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and spasms that can lead to excessive gas and burping. Additionally, lemon balm's carminative properties help to ease digestion and eliminate trapped air in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother, more efficient movement of food and reducing the frequency and intensity of unwanted belches. This natural remedy provides relief from uncomfortable burping without harsh chemicals or artificial additives.
remedy_properties: Digestive Soothing Properties: Lemon balm tincture's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic compounds help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and discomfort that can lead to burping.

Carminative Action: The volatile oils present in lemon balm tincture have natural carminative properties, which help to release trapped gas from the digestive system, reducing the frequency and intensity of burping.
Antacid Properties: Lemon balm's antacid compounds can help to neutralize excess stomach acid, reducing symptoms like heartburn and bloating that can contribute to burping.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system, alleviating burping and discomfort.
Linalool: This terpene has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and inflammation that can cause burping and indigestion.
Linalyl acetate: Similar to linalool, it has a sedative effect on the digestive system, helping to calm and soothe the stomach and intestines, reducing burping and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tinctures due to their antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties.
Flowers: They are used to make tinctures due to their calming and antispasmodic effects.
Leaves: They are also used in herbal remedies for digestive issues, and their tincture is sometimes used to aid in digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated and dry them in a dark place.
Combine 1 part dried melissa leaves and flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking it daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and discard the cheesecloth.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with burping because its essential oils, particularly anethole, have a natural anti-gas property that soothes digestive issues. When ingested, fennel tincture can ease bloating and reduce gas production in the stomach and intestines, leading to a significant decrease in belching. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing trapped gases to pass through more efficiently, thereby minimizing burping.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel tincture's carminative properties help to relieve bloating, gas, and discomfort in the digestive tract by reducing the formation of gas and its movement through the intestines.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in fennel tincture reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to excessive burping and alleviate associated symptoms such as abdominal pain and cramping.

Spasmolytic properties: Fennel tincture's spasmolytic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, including the esophagus and stomach, which reduces the frequency and intensity of belching and bloating.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole, a phenolic compound, helps reduce gas and alleviate symptoms of burping by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing inflammation.
Fenchone: Fenchone, a terpene, aids in relieving burping by inhibiting the production of gas-producing bacteria in the gut and improving digestion.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, in Foeniculum vulgare tinctures help alleviate burping by reducing inflammation, improving gut health, and inhibiting the growth of gas-producing bacteria.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their carminative properties, which help to ease digestive discomfort and reduce burping.
Seeds: They contain compounds that help to relax the digestive muscles and reduce gas, thereby alleviating burping.
Stems: They are used for their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the digestive system and reduce burping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems on a sunny day.
Chop 1 cup of the harvested material into small pieces for easy extraction.
Combine 1 cup of chopped foeniculum vulgare with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill tinctures helps with burping because of its natural antispasmodic and carminative properties. The essential oils present in dill, such as carvone and limonene, help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating. This can effectively reduce the frequency and intensity of belching, providing relief from discomfort and embarrassment. remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used to make tinctures for burping due to their carminative properties, which help to alleviate gas and indigestion.
Leaves: Anethum graveolens leaves are also used to make tinctures for burping as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and carminative effects, helping to soothe digestive issues.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried anethum graveolens flowers and 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the dried flowers in the vodka for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in the refrigerator.
Label and date the bottle and use the tincture within 2 years of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with burping because of its natural soothing properties that calm digestive issues. The root extract in herbal licorice tinctures has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce irritation and discomfort in the stomach, alleviating symptoms like bloating and gas. Additionally, licorice's natural carminative properties help to expel trapped air from the digestive system, effectively silencing excessive burping. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, which can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating.

Soothing properties: The saponins present in licorice root have a soothing effect on mucous membranes, which can help calm digestive issues like heartburn and burping by reducing irritation and discomfort.

Licoricidin: Licoricidin, a phenolic glycoside, may help with burping by inhibiting the growth of bacteria in the gut, reducing digestive inflammation, and promoting a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_parts: Roots: As the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a key compound in licorice, roots are used to create tinctures that help alleviate digestive issues, including burping.
Leaves: Containing a similar composition to roots, leaves are used to make tinctures that provide similar benefits, including soothing digestive issues and reducing burping.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help in reducing inflammation and alleviating digestive issues, including burping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the glycyrrhiza glabra root in the autumn season when the plant is mature.
Dry the harvested root in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind 50 grams of the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 50 grams of the powdered root with 250 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks then strain and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers on a sunny day when they are in full bloom.

Steep 1 part of the dried flowers in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Often used in herbal remedies, the leaves of Artemisia dracunculus may be used to make tinctures, but I couldn't find any information on its use for burping.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 pounds of fresh or dried artemisia dracunculus leaves and flowers in clean containers.
Chop 1 pound of the gathered plant material into small pieces using sharp knives or a grinder.
Combine 1 pound of chopped plant material with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking it daily to aid extraction.
Strain the liquid using a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh dioscorea villosa roots and wash them thoroughly in cold running water.

Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks allowing the tincture to macerate and extract bioactive compounds.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh or dried licorice root extract of glycyrrhiza uralensis containing 20-30% glycyrrhetic acid.
Grind the harvested material into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part powdered licorice root with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar for a 1:2 ratio.
Store the mixture at room temperature in a dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar and discard the solids after filtering for 10 minutes.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome, a thick underground stem, is often used to make medicinal tinctures because it contains a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm digestive issues like excessive burping.

Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are also used to make tinctures, as they contain a rich source of volatile oils and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and burping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis aristata roots dig them clean and chop them into small pieces.
Combine 100 grams of chopped roots with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place away from direct sunlight.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the tincture in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and use within one year.

Isoberberine: Isoberberine is another active compound found in goldenseal that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms like belching and discomfort.

Hydrophobic compounds: Goldenseal contains a range of hydrophobic (water-repelling) compounds, including berberine and isoberberine, which can help to calm and soothe the digestive system, reducing the production of gas and burping.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried goldenseal roots in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and chop 100-200 grams of goldenseel roots into small pieces for maceration.
Combine chopped roots with 500 ml of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days to allow for extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.
